title: Dracaena caulescens
taxonomy: Dracaena caulescens is classified within the family Asparagaceae. More specifically, it belongs to the subfamily Nolinoideae. This classification is based on the plant's morphological and molecular characteristics. The genus Dracaena, to which it belongs, is a group of flowering plants commonly known as dragon trees or dragon plants. The classification of Dracaena caulescens reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Asparagaceae family.

intro: Dracaena caulescens, also known as the dragon tree or dragon plant, is a plant species within the Asparagaceae family, native to the tropical regions of Asia and Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can aid in the treatment of various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Dracaena caulescens include saponins, flavonoids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which are used to treat various ailments and promote wound healing.
